title: Translate Research into a Prioritized Design Brief
summary: Translate Research into a Prioritized Design Brief retrieves prior skills in an original resident heat-relief research synthesis scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply design user goals in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real resident heat-relief research synthesis artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

options.text: Resident heat-relief research synthesis case 1: treat every requested feature as equally urgent so no stakeholder preference is challenged.

Resident heat-relief research synthesis case 1: separate requests from research evidence, state prioritized user tasks and constraints, and define observable success before choosing interface features.
Resident heat-relief research synthesis case 1: choose colors and components first because visual polish reveals the correct user task.

steps.title: 04 · Read the resident heat-relief research synthesis contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: resident heat-relief research synthesis.
steps.why: Translate Research into a Prioritized Design Brief makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in resident heat-relief research synthesis.

content.text: A stakeholder request is an input, not proof of a user need. A design brief names people, jobs, contexts, constraints, risks, assumptions, and observable success evidence. Prioritized goals prevent a feature list from competing equally and let the team test whether a resident can complete the actual service task. Apply it to resident heat-relief research synthesis.

Resident heat-relief research synthesis case 4: the stakeholder feature list is equivalent to evidence about user needs.
